nameMeanings.name: Crawford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various places called Crawford, primarily the one in Lanarkshire (Scotland), and possibly also from the one in Lancashire. Both are named in Old English with crāwe ‘crow’ + ford ‘ford’.

variants.name: Crafford
meanings: English (Cumberland) and Scottish: variant of   Crawford  .

variants.name: Crofoot
meanings: English: variant ofCrowfoot, a nickname for someone with splayed feet or someother deformity of the foot, from Old English crāwe ‘crow’ +fōt ‘foot’. In Middle English crou-fot also denotedthe buttercup, and it may be from this sense that the name arose,although the reason for its adoption is unclear.

variants.name: Crofut
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of Crawford or Crowfoot (see Crofoot ).

nameMeanings.name: Janet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

variants.name: Jannet
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Galbraith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: ethnic name for a Strathclyde Briton, from Gaelic gall Breathnach ‘British foreigner’. Compare Gall .

History: This name is first recorded as a surname in the Lennox, a rich agricultural region north of Glasgow under the Campsie Fells, in the 12th century. In early medieval times the region to which Lennox belongs was an independent Welsh-speaking kingdom, with its capital at Dumbarton. It was not integrated into the rest of Scotland until 1124. The first recorded chief of the Galbraiths was Gilchrist ‘the Briton’, living in 1193.

variants.name: Galbreth
meanings: Scottish (Argyll): variant of   Galbraith  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gilbreth
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Galbraith  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gall
meanings: Short form of   Gillian  , rather less frequent than   Jill .

variants.name: Galbreath
meanings: Scottish (Renfrewshire and Argyll): variant of   Galbraith  .

variants.name: Gilbreath
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Galbraith  . This surname is now very rare in Britain.

en.lifeSummary: When Janet Galbraith Crawford was born in 1350, in Fishcross, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, her father, Malcolm Crawford, was 31 and her mother, Margaret Galbraith, was 15. She had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Sir Alexander de la Schaw. She died about 1390, in Greenock, Renfrewshire, Scotland, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Janet Galbraith Crawford est née en 1350, à Fishcross, Clackmannanshire, Écosse, son père, Malcolm Crawford, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margaret Galbraith, avait 15 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Alexander de la Schaw. Elle est décédée environ 1390, à Greenock, Renfrewshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Janet Galbraith Crawford 1350 in Fishcross, Clackmannanshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Malcolm Crawford 31 und ihre Mutter Margaret Galbraith 15. Sie hatte mit Sir Alexander de la Schaw mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1390 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Greenock, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Janet Galbraith Crawford è nata il nel 1350 nel Fishcross, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, sua padre, Malcolm Crawford, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Margaret Galbraith, aveva 15 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Sir Alexander de la Schaw. Morì circa 1390, nel Greenock, Renfrewshire, Scotland, all'età di 41 anni.
